input:
He went into the mountains and stayed until the Prophet and his companions departed. When he went back home, he found a letter left him by his brother, al-Walīd ibn al-Walīd, who had been a Muslim for some time. Al-Walīd was in the Prophet’s company when he came to Makkah for his compensatory ʿUmrah. After completing the duties of the ʿUmrah, al-Walīd tried to find his brother Khālid. When he could not find him, he realized that Khālid was trying to run away from Islam.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 32: A Second Trip to Makkah

output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 32: A Second Trip to Makkah

characters.name: Khalid ibn al-Walid
characters.appearance: Early 20s, athletic build, short beard, slightly dark olive skin tone. Wearing simple beige thobe, no cloak, off-white cloth wrapped as head covering, worn leather sandals. Slightly tense posture, looking around nervously.

characters.name: Al-Walid ibn al-Walid
characters.appearance: Mid 20s, medium build, slightly dark olive skin tone, short beard with slight wave. Wearing simple beige thobe, no cloak, off-white cloth wrapped as head covering, worn leather sandals. Slightly tired and worried expression, holding a letter.

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

output.moral_lesson: The importance of perseverance in one's faith and the support of family and friends.
output.narrative_text: Al-Walid returned home from a trip to find a letter from his brother Khalid, who had become a Muslim. However, when Al-Walid arrived in Makkah, he couldn't find Khalid, and realized he was trying to run away from Islam.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, Al-Walid and Khalid shown with full faces, no graphic violence.

output.image_prompt: Watercolor illustration of a 7th century Makkah mud-brick home, with a man in his mid 20s, dark olive skin, short beard, wearing beige thobe and off-white cloth head covering, sitting on a woven mat looking worried, holding a letter. In the background, a man in his early 20s, athletic build, short beard, wearing beige thobe and off-white cloth head covering, looking around nervously. A man in a simple white thobe with a green cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing in the distance, at the edge of the scene. Simple mud-brick walls, clay water jug in corner. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.
